version.release_notes:
## TrueNAS 25.10.4

This maintenance release updates the Linux kernel and Samba to address multiple security vulnerabilities, and includes ZFS data integrity, NFS, iSCSI, networking, and Active Directory fixes.

- **Security:** Updates the Linux kernel to v6.12.91 and Samba to 4.22.10, mitigating multiple CVEs. Kernel updates include the Dirty-Frag local privilege escalation, a ptrace privilege issue, and the related Fragnesia ESP-in-TCP privilege escalation. Samba updates address a missing access check that let read-only users alter reparse point attributes and a WORM module flaw that allowed protected files to be overwritten through rename.
- **Critical Bug Fixes:** Fixes a potential ZFS double free when freeing blocks cloned after deduplication table pruning, virtual machines stored on NFSv4.1 datasets failing to power on under VMware ESXi, and a kernel crash in the iSCSI target during SCSI bus or LUN resets on Enterprise High Availability (HA) systems.
- **HA and Networking Improvements:** Improves iSCSI LUN replacement during HA failover by holding cleanup until cluster coordination completes, and resolves a validation error that incorrectly blocked static network configuration on HA-capable systems when DHCP was not being enabled.
- **Active Directory Improvements:** Hardens directory rejoin, reset, and recovery handling, improves domain controller selection on systems with multiple controllers, and produces clearer diagnostics when a join or authentication problem occurs.
- **Additional Fixes:** Resolves ZFS automatic snapshots not being created after a Time Machine backup until the Mac reconnects on macOS Tahoe and later, and reduces excessive winbind log warnings for failed user and group lookups.

**Pre-Update Actions:** None required when updating from 25.10.3.1.

**[Version Recommendations](https://www.truenas.com/software-status/)** | **[Full Release Notes](https://www.truenas.com/docs/scale/25.10/gettingstarted/versionnotes/#25.10.4)** | **[Known Issues](https://www.truenas.com/docs/scale/25.10/gettingstarted/versionnotes/#known-issues)** | **[Enterprise Support](https://support.truenas.com/)** | **[Forums](https://forums.truenas.com/)**

## TrueNAS 25.10.4

This maintenance release updates the Linux kernel and Samba to address multiple security vulnerabilities, and includes ZFS data integrity, NFS, iSCSI, networking, and Active Directory fixes.

- **Security:** Updates the Linux kernel to v6.12.91 and Samba to 4.22.10, mitigating multiple CVEs. Kernel updates include the Dirty-Frag local privilege escalation, a ptrace privilege issue, and the related Fragnesia ESP-in-TCP privilege escalation. Samba updates address a missing access check that let read-only users alter reparse point attributes and a WORM module flaw that allowed protected files to be overwritten through rename.
- **Critical Bug Fixes:** Fixes a potential ZFS double free when freeing blocks cloned after deduplication table pruning, virtual machines stored on NFSv4.1 datasets failing to power on under VMware ESXi, and a kernel crash in the iSCSI target during SCSI bus or LUN resets on Enterprise High Availability (HA) systems.
- **HA and Networking Improvements:** Improves iSCSI LUN replacement during HA failover by holding cleanup until cluster coordination completes, and resolves a validation error that incorrectly blocked static network configuration on HA-capable systems when DHCP was not being enabled.
- **Active Directory Improvements:** Hardens directory rejoin, reset, and recovery handling, improves domain controller selection on systems with multiple controllers, and produces clearer diagnostics when a join or authentication problem occurs.
- **Additional Fixes:** Resolves ZFS automatic snapshots not being created after a Time Machine backup until the Mac reconnects on macOS Tahoe and later, and reduces excessive winbind log warnings for failed user and group lookups.

**Pre-Update Actions:** None required when updating from 25.10.3.1.

**[Version Recommendations](https://www.truenas.com/software-status/)** | **[Full Release Notes](https://www.truenas.com/docs/scale/25.10/gettingstarted/versionnotes/#25.10.4)** | **[Known Issues](https://www.truenas.com/docs/scale/25.10/gettingstarted/versionnotes/#known-issues)** | **[Enterprise Support](https://support.truenas.com/)** | **[Forums](https://forums.truenas.com/)**

capabilities.description: Transmission is able to change file ownership arbitrarily
capabilities.name: CHOWN

capabilities.description: Transmission is able to bypass file permission checks
capabilities.name: DAC_OVERRIDE

capabilities.description: Transmission is able to bypass permission checks for file operations
capabilities.name: FOWNER

capabilities.description: Transmission is able to change group ID of processes
capabilities.name: SETGID

capabilities.description: Transmission is able to change user ID of processes
capabilities.name: SETUID

metadata.description: Transmission is designed for easy, powerful use.
metadata.name: transmission

run_as_context.description: Container [transmission] runs as root user and group.

metadata.title: Transmission

apps.notes:
# Transmission

## Security

**Read the following security precautions to ensure that you wish to continue using this application.**

---

### Container: [transmission]

#### Running user/group(s)

- User: root
- Group: root
- Supplementary Groups: apps

---

For additional configuration you can either use the WebUI,
or add them in the [additional_config] section.

See [available options](https://github.com/transmission/transmission/blob/main/docs/Editing-Configuration-Files.md#options).
Anything set on the [additional_config] section will be added/updated to the `settings.json`
and changing it's value from the WebUI will be reset on the next deployment.

## Bug Reports and Feature Requests

If you find a bug in this app or have an idea for a new feature, please file an issue at
https://github.com/truenas/apps

# Jellyfin

## Security

**Read the following security precautions to ensure that you wish to continue using this application.**

---

### Container: [jellyfin]

#### Running user/group(s)

- User: 568
- Group: 568
- Supplementary Groups: apps

#### Passing Host Files, Devices, or Sockets into the Container

- DRI Device (/dev/dri) - (Read/Write)

---

### Container: [permissions]

**This container is short-lived.**

#### Running user/group(s)

- User: root
- Group: root
- Supplementary Groups: apps

---

## Bug Reports and Feature Requests

If you find a bug in this app or have an idea for a new feature, please file an issue at
https://github.com/truenas/apps

apps.name: traefik

metadata.description: An application for deploying simple containers.
metadata.name: ix-app
metadata.title: iX App

apps.notes:
# iX App

## Security

**Read the following security precautions to ensure that you wish to continue using this application.**

---

### Container: [traefik]

#### Running user/group(s)

- User: unknown
- Group: unknown
- Supplementary Groups: apps

#### Security option [no-new-privileges] is not set

- Processes can gain additional privileges through setuid/setgid binaries
- Can potentially allow privilege escalation attacks within the container

---



## Bug Reports and Feature Requests

If you find a bug in this app or have an idea for a new feature, please file an issue at
https://github.com/truenas/apps
